ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Di obe wɔ di Yesu labuo ni Yerusalem, kudu niamu di okpoo omuu. Ni baatii latɔɔkaalɛ alɛ, “Owe dile?”
translated: They were on their way to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them. The disciples were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. They took the twelve aside again and began to tell them what was going to happen to him, saying, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death and deliver him over to the Gentiles. And they will mock him and spit on him and flog him and kill him. But after three days he will rise.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Diekye Otii Obi nwu nkpo kamawa di Ote lɛkpa kamɛ ku Yaa batɔkyɛntɛ nɔɔ, abata otii biala koto di nkpa nɔɔ be eesiɛ ni kanya.
translated: The chief, Otii Obi, was worried about the strange occurrence at Ote, where a huge python had been killed, and Yaa, a young girl, was implicated; she was brought to the chief's court to explain what had happened.

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Nioso osuɔtɔ nwu lasifi aalaabuɛ lɛsaa le di Yesu labla aatɔɔ ni diɛ ekpoo nwu lefosi aafenko. Diabla batii ba nianu ni ɔkpɛ. Yesu Layɔɔsa Ɔnɔɔfo Yairo Obi Sankobi Ku Ɔsanko Onwii Ofiɛ.
translated: The one who does not love does not know God, because God is love. This is how God showed his love among us: He sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him. This is love: not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as an atoning sacrifice for our sins. Dear friends, since God so loved us, we also ought to love one another. No one has ever seen God; but if we love one another, God lives in us and his love is made complete in us.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Obe wɔ aakyɛ ni sitũ kɛɛkɛ, ni aakple aanya Yakobo ku obilɛma Yohane ninle ni Sebedeo babi nɛ. Baasiɛ di oklo lɛma kamɛ manko ote lɛma baatooloso asawu lɛma. Ni di Yesu lalɛɛ ma nɛ.
translated: He saw two brothers, Simon Peter and Andrew, casting a net into the sea; and they were fishermen. He said to them, "Follow me, and I will make you fishers of people."

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: “Nunua bia yi, batɔɔyɔ kafɛ beese di awoso sidu suoto kofokofoko. Kowoso lele ninantika abi biene yi, bababudi kũ, man-yɔ kũ mankpee di ɔtɔ kamɛ.
translated: Buy beer, let's go and drink it together under the shade of the tree. When we are there let us discuss our problems, and plan how we can solve them.

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: bu teefɔ ku yaafɔ, bɔmbɔ owo lɛfɔ, fɛ mmle okle fambɔmbɔ ni suoto lɛfɔ.”
translated: “You will take care of the farm, and I will take care of the house, we will work together to build a better life.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Kakyɛ nwu nkpo Yosef lakpaa obisɔ nwu nkpo ku ɔya baatoso baasifi Egipte kasɔ suoto.
translated: The brothers killed an animal and dipped Joseph's coat in the blood, then they took it to their father.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Nkpo oso aamunikĩi aatɔkɔ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ, “Ofiɛɛlɛ nwu mpɔ, kafɔɔ bafiɛlɛtɛ diɛpɔ.
translated: The elders of the village gathered under a tree and said, “Our daughter is spoiled, who will take her?”

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Baadiki sikpile lɛma baabuɛ, ni aakpeesa ma Yaa ntu di Yordan okle kamɛ nɛ.
translated: The people of Sikkim are very happy, and they are celebrating with Yaa, who has come from Jordan.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: “Ni biɛta batii ba ninkpe ni di Yudia batoso basifi abokote osi balaawofa.
translated: They are people who do not know that the Jews are the ones they should fear.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Nse sɛka kalekɔ kawo lɛsɛ kɛtaka keeyila di suoto nɔɔ, ni sɛka kalekɔ nwu dilabafuo oyila.
translated: The dog chases the cat up the tree and the cat climbs up to the top.

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, batii bawo nsi ye kaamɛ ninfũ, blɔwa ɔbakpi fiɛ babanya Otii Obi nwu nkpo kɔwa fɛ ɔka.”
translated: The child was crying, his mother beat him and sent him to fetch firewood, but he ran to Obi's place and said "My mother is beating me".

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Ɔmɛmbe faabuo wo kɔfɔɔ buafũ fɔ ku otu biene, ee faakyɛ lɛkyɔtɔ buata fɔ asaa faaklee?
translated: What good is a bag of money to someone who has no mouth to eat with?

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Ɔlaa wɔ mmle lakpɛ kuakyaka kasɔ nwu nkpo aba aba. Yesu Latikiti Banumbiɛtɛ Banyɔ Banwii Anu.
translated: The children are playing outside, and the noise they are making is disturbing me. Jesus Christ, help me.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu latɔkɔ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ, “Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, dibawɛ osie fiɛ di basaawɛntɛ makyɛ Yaa sɛka kalekɔ nwu nkpo.
translated: One day Jesus was teaching the people in the temple, “The scribes and the Pharisees have seated themselves in Moses’ chair;"

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Difu lapɛ ɔsanko nwu, diekye aatofo lɛsaa le niɛwa ni di suoto kamɛ, nioso aayɔ lɛyɛkɛ ku ditũtũkũ aalaapɛ akunkyi di Yesu nkpaatũ ni aatɔkɔ nwɔ nwaako wɔ ninsi ni di ɔlaa nwu kamɛ nɛ.
translated: The devil is an enemy, the one who opposes the good news, and does not want to hear the word and does not want to do the things that please God, because he does not have the love of God in him, and does not want to follow Jesus Christ and does not want to be saved.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: “Otii wɔ nimafũ ni obisɔ biibii wɔ mmle okle di leyooto nii kamɛ yi, ami eefũ. Tannanfi Otii Wo Lɛfɔ Faakpee Di Okpile Kamɛ.
translated: The Otii are knowledgeable and have a deep understanding of the customs and traditions of their people, and they are respected for their wisdom. They are the custodians of the Okpile Kamɛ sacred tradition.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Nyu mmle di miɛ akyɛnfɔɔle lɛbla ni batii kpile, batikanko ni afiɔ ɔtɔ lo! Biomiɛ bilɛ mbla dituosaa, kafɔɔ, namabla. Dituosaa le lete babadiki mantuo ye minnya ni ninle Yaa Ɔlaa Buɛtɛtɛ Yona lele le okle nɛ.
translated: The women of Jerusalem said to him, “You have done this to us on the day of our celebration, on the day of our dance, on the day of our joy! What shall we do to you, our dear one, our sweet one? Our dear one, tell us what to do to you, now that you have done this to us on the day of our festival, on the day of our jubilation.”

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: Ni baayɔ kataabookyɛɛ baapɛ baakaasa di lɛkantosi nɔɔ, ni baakpana lɛsaa le oso beelo nwɔ nii alɛ, “Nnya Yesu Yuda Batii Ɔka Nwu Ninle Nwɔ Nɛ.”
translated: They were saying to one another, “No one knows where he is; perhaps he has gone to be a guest with the disciples and said, “I am going to be with you Jews at Passover; I will celebrate the Passover with you.”

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: “Ninfɛ di banumbentɛ nwu nkpo latɔkɔ ma alɛ, ‘Oo, nnɔfɔ be bukle ni dilabawo awo ku aye. Nkpo oso bitɔɔkyɛ basunsutɛ nfũ biɛlaaya.’
translated: “The ninfee bird does not abandon its nest when it rains, ‘Oh, the wind will surely blow and the clouds will scatter. The nest will surely be destroyed.’
